laws.title: Public Law 19-144 | Public Area Management Act (PAMA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute regulating road/sidewalk closures in DC by various agencies; SEC9 repeals specified subsections of a prior public law (PL 15-95). No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'PAMA'; severability; effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Public Area Management Act' or 'PAMA'."

provisions.text_summary: Purpose and definitions (district, public/private road, armed forces, national guard).
provisions.evidence: "The purpose of this act is to prevent abuse by law enforcement in closing roads and sidewalks..."

provisions.text_summary: Closure authority for armed forces, National Guard, Secret Service, Capitol Police, FPS, Marshals, FBI, DSS, DC Fire/EMS, MPD/FPS; bar on closing roads around the gun store.
provisions.evidence: "When deployed in the district by the President of the United States, the armed forces shall have the authority to close any public roads..."
provisions.notes: References PL 17-136 (Parks Act) and PL 17-126 (Special Events Act) as in force.

provisions.text_summary: Closure reasons and limitations (public health/safety/national security; 24-hour limit; citizen re-entry).
provisions.evidence: "Any road closure must be made in the interest of public health, safety, national security..."

provisions.text_summary: Repeals PL 15-95 Section VI(2), (3), and (4) in their entirety.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 15-95, Section VI(2)(3) and (4) shall be repealed in their entirety."

current_implementation.detail: Draft inserted only a boilerplate note at the Title 54 anchor and did not make the repeal-marking or permanent textual edits the source requires. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to PL 15-95 §VI(2)-(4).
Codify the closure authority and limitation rules as actual text, not a note.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-145 | Military Officer Accountability Act (MOAA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Repeals-and-reenacts multiple 10 U.S. Code Chapter 60 officer-separation sections (§§1181, 1182(a), 1184, 1185(a)(1), 1186(a), 1187(b), 1161) with determinable replacement text. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'MOAA'; severability; effective date; PL 1-1 general provision.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Military Officer Accountability Act' or 'MOAA'."

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1181 repealed and re-enacted (review of officer records; show-cause grounds).
provisions.exact_change: Repeal §1181 in its entirety and replace with the SEC5(1) text.
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§ 1181 shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1181 shall now state;"

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1182(a) repealed and re-enacted (boards of inquiry).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§1182(a) shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1182(a) shall now state;"

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1184 repealed and re-enacted (removal from active duty on board recommendation).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§ 1184 shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1184 shall now state;"

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1185(a)(1) repealed and re-enacted (72-hour written notice).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§ 1185(a)(1) shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1185(a)(1) shall now state;"

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1186(a) repealed and re-enacted (voluntary retirement/discharge on request).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§1186(a) shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1186(a)(1) shall now state;"
provisions.notes: Source labels the replacement '(a)(1)' though the repeal cites '(a)'.

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1187(b) repealed and re-enacted (board composition when insufficient officers).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§1187(b) shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1187(b) shall now read as;"

provisions.text_summary: 10 U.S. Code §1161 repealed and re-enacted (dismissal of commissioned officers; drop-from-rolls grounds; President authority).
provisions.evidence: "10U.S.Code§ 1161 shall be repealed in its entirety. 10U.S.Code§1161 shall now read as;"

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t10/s101/h and declared 'Current'; it made no textual amendments to 10 USC 1161, 1181, 1182, 1184, 1185, 1186, or 1187. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Amend-existing-text for 10 USC 1161, 1181, 1182(a), 1184, 1185(a)(1), 1186(a), and 1187(b) with the SEC5-6 replacement text.
Remove Trello link; re-anchor notes to the affected sections.

laws.title: Public Law 19-147 | Diplomatic Standards Act (DSA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute adapting the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations for the US (missions, persona non grata, immunities, waivers); SEC17 repeals 8 U.S. Code §1102 in its entirety. No self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'DSA'; severability; effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Diplomatic Standards Act' or 'DSA'."

provisions.text_summary: Purpose (adapt Vienna Convention 1969) and definitions.
provisions.evidence: "to amend existing US law and adapt the provisions of the Vienna convention on diplomatic relations, 1969..."

provisions.text_summary: Functions of a mission; notification/objection; persona non grata; notification obligations; credentials; vacancy of mission head; flag use; inviolability of premises; freedom of movement; protection/immunities of diplomats; immunity waiver.
provisions.evidence: "The functions of a diplomatic mission consist, inter alia, of: (a) Representing the sending State in the United States..."
provisions.notes: Substantial permanent general law; candidate for new Title 22 sections.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals 8 U.S. Code §1102 in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: "8U.S.Code§1102 shall be repealed in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Sequencing: PL-17-127 SEC8 earlier rewrote §1102; this later Act repeals it.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t8/s1101/i/2 and declared 'Repealed'; it failed to effect the SEC17 repeal of 8 USC 1102 and did not codify the substantial diplomatic-standards provisions. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to 8 USC 1102 (SEC17), sequenced after PL-17-127's rewrite.
Codify SEC6-16 as new Title 22 diplomatic-relations sections or a consolidated statutory note.
Drop unsupported blanket 'repealed' status for the whole Act.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-148 | Badges and Entrapment Act (BEA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute requiring officers to display badges and establishing entrapment as a defense; references 18 U.S. Code §1515(a)(4) as a definitional cross-cite but amends no numbered Code section.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'BEA'; severability; effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Badges and Entrapment Act' or 'BEA'."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (badge, entrapment, officer, government official).
provisions.evidence: "The term 'officer' means any member of a law enforcement, or, persons defined in 18 U.S.Code.§1515(a)(4)."

provisions.text_summary: All officers must display a badge in performance of duties, except where it would endanger life, compromise a sensitive/classified investigation, or an authorized undercover operation.
provisions.evidence: "All officers must display a badge when in performance of their official duties, expect in situations where..."

provisions.text_summary: Entrapment as an official defense; two elements; dismissals; penalties for officers who entrap or proceed knowing of entrapment.
provisions.evidence: "Entrapment shall be an official defence in criminal trials. It shall have two elements to it;"
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 18 section.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t50/s1 and declared 'Current'. The substantive criminal-procedure law warrants a Title 18 section. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify SEC4-6 as new Title 18 sections (badge display + entrapment defense) rather than a Title 50 note.
Remove Trello link; correct placement.

laws.title: Public Law 19-149 | Criminal Justice Reform Act (CJRA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. SEC5 rewrites 18 U.S. Code §3156(a)(3) (felony definition); SEC6 repeals a prior public law (PL 2-5); SEC7 resets maximum sentences for numerous Title 18 offenses/chapters. No self-repeal.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'CJRA'; severability; effective date; PL 1-1 general provision.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Criminal Justice Reform Act' or 'CJRA'."

provisions.text_summary: 18 U.S. Code §3156(a)(3) rewritten: 'felony' means an offence punishable by more than eight days imprisonment.
provisions.exact_change: Replace §3156(a)(3) with 'The term "felony" shall mean an offence punishable by a maximum term of imprisonment of more than eight days;'.
provisions.evidence: "18U.S.Code§3156(a)(3) shall now read as-"

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 2-5 in its entirety.
provisions.evidence: "Public Law 2-5 shall be repealed in its entirety."

provisions.text_summary: Resets maximum imprisonment for numerous Title 18 sections and chapter-wide/catch-all limits.
provisions.exact_change: Amend each cited section's maximum-sentence text to the specified day-limit; apply chapter-wide 40-day and catch-all 7-day limits.
provisions.evidence: "The maximum imprisonment time for U.S.Code §1111, Murder, shall not exceed nine days."
provisions.notes: Determinable per-section limits; the catch-all is a blanket instruction to be noted.

provisions.text_summary: Accumulated-sentence cap of 40 days; judges barred from exceeding it; impeachment liability for violators.
provisions.evidence: "No Judge may issue a Judgement that constitutes a sentence longer than forty days..."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t18/s1 and declared 'Repealed'; it made no textual amendments to §3156 or the SEC7 penalty sections and did not effect the PL 2-5 repeal. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Amend-existing-text for 18 USC 3156(a)(3) and each SEC7-named section's maximum penalty; record the chapter-wide and catch-all limits as statutory notes where non-section-specific.
Apply repeal-marking to PL 2-5 (SEC6).
Drop unsupported whole-Act 'repealed' status.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-150 | Small Businesses Empowerment Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present (HTML-rendered PDF). Free-standing statute directing the Department of Commerce and Labor to establish small-business recognition, employment, and support programs. Administrative/programmatic; no self-repeal or sunset. Body short title reads 'Small Business Empowerment and Support Act'.

provisions.text_summary: Short title (body: 'Small Business Empowerment and Support Act').
provisions.evidence: "This Act may be cited as the 'Small Business Empowerment and Support Act.'"
provisions.notes: Manifest/list title says 'Small Businesses Empowerment Act'; body differs.

provisions.text_summary: Purpose and definitions (small business, recognition, employment initiatives, support/advisory services).
provisions.evidence: "Small businesses are crucial for community economic prosperity..."

provisions.text_summary: DOCAL shall establish a Small Business Recognition Program.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall establish a Small Business Recognition Program..."

provisions.text_summary: DOCAL shall initiate a Small Business Employment Initiative.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall initiate a Small Business Employment Initiative..."

provisions.text_summary: DOCAL shall establish a Small Business Support Center.
provisions.evidence: "The Department shall establish a Small Business Support Center within the Department of Commerce and Labor."

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately upon passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall take effect immediately upon passage through both houses of Congress and upon the signature of the President."

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t15/s1 and declared 'Current'. The programmatic DOCAL directives are not implemented as text. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify the DOCAL small-business programs as a Title 15 section or concise statutory note.
Reconcile the short-title discrepancy (body vs. manifest) in editorial notes.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 19-151 | Court Fees Prohibition and Protection Act (CTPPA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. SEC4 repeals enumerated 28 U.S. Code court-fee sections in their entirety; SEC5 adds a free-standing prohibition on court fees. No self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'CTPPA'; severability; effective date.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as 'Court Fees Prohibition and Protection Act' or 'CTPPA'"

provisions.exact_change: Repeal-mark each listed section (§1918 only subsection (b)); §1932 listed twice, so de-duplicate.
provisions.evidence: "28U.S.Code§1911 shall be repealed in its entirety."
provisions.notes: Overlaps with PL-17-133 repeals of §1913, §1920-1922, §1926, and §1930.

provisions.text_summary: No court may impose or collect fees except as punishment for a criminal offence or contempt; fees set by Congress.
provisions.evidence: "No Court in the United States, including the Supreme Court, shall impose, collect, or otherwise require fees in any of its proceedings unless imposed as a punishment for a criminal offence or contempt of court."
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 28 section.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t28/s1 and declared 'Current'; it made no repeal-markings for the enumerated Title 28 fee sections and did not codify the SEC5 prohibition. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Apply repeal-marking to each SEC4 section (subsection §1918(b) only), de-duplicating §1932 and reconciling with PL-17-133's overlapping repeals.
Codify SEC5 fee prohibition as a new Title 28 section.
Remove Trello link.
